content.full_text:
November 7, 1977

Mr. Duncan M. James  
Post Office Box 1000  
Ukiah, California 95482  

Dear Mr. James:

This letter is to confirm our conversation of November 2, 1977, in which I advised you that you should disclose a $500 payment from the D. A.'s Association as a gift. This is because the money was provided to you for the purpose of taking your wife to the National D. A.'s Association convention. It was not given to you as payment or compensation for services which you rendered to the D. A.'s Association and, therefore, should not be disclosed as income.

If you have any further questions, please feel free to contact me.

Sincerely,

Ted Prim  
Chief, Conflicts of Interest Division

TP:ma
